label: SES-063 PI-028 commit entity schema spec authored: status-free documentary lifecycle settled (DEC-198), FK-over-references-edge deviation articulated (DEC-199), four new cross-spec precedents established (DEC-200); 1 session, 3 decisions, 4 references. Rebased at close-out time from SES-062 / DEC-191..193 to SES-063 / DEC-198..200 after a parallel sandbox session pushed ses_062.json (PI-025 prior-conversations backfill, DEC-191..197) first. PI-029 schema migration prompt A authored at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-pi-029-A-commits-table-and-vocab.md and queued for Claude Code execution. PI-028 stays Open per methodology §9 — resolves_planning_items payload section does not yet ship.

session.title: Commit entity schema spec authored per the seven-section governance schema-spec format; PI-029 schema-migration prompt A queued
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai ARCHITECTURE-mode schema-design conversation. Opened against the PI-028 kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-commit.md to render the v0.8 commit entity type defined in methodology-code-change-lifecycle.md §3.1 into the standard nine-subsection schema-spec format and produce the first Claude Code prompt for the downstream PI-029 schema migration. The conversation was heavily pre-decided by the methodology document (eight foundation decisions DEC-183 through DEC-190 had already settled the architectural questions); this conversation's substantive contribution was the labeling of inheritable precedents and the articulation of the FK-over-references-edge deviation from DEC-124 the methodology had silently adopted. Three decisions authored: DEC-198 locks the status-free documentary lifecycle as a refinement of DEC-137; DEC-199 articulates the FK-over-references-edge deviation with frequency-justified-denormalization rationale; DEC-200 elevates four new cross-spec precedents for successor governance specs to inherit.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Project default operating mode: ARCHITECTURE." [project preamble], followed by the PI-028 kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-commit.md attached to the opening turn. Substantive task: author PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-schema-specs/commit.md per the seven-section format defined in governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md, settling the seven design questions enumerated in the kickoff.

Structured summary of the schema-design work:

(1) Identifier rebasing at orientation, then again at close. The kickoff (authored 05-23-26 21:00 by SES-061) anticipated this conversation closing at SES-061 itself per the kickoff close-out-expectations block. In the intervening window SES-060 (audit-v1.2 planning, DEC-178..182) and SES-061 (methodology drafting, DEC-183..190) had been authored and their payloads queued in close-out-payloads/. At conversation start, actual heads verified against the CRMBUILDER db-export snapshots showed SES-059 as highest applied session and ses_060.json / ses_061.json queued, so this conversation planned to close at SES-062 with decisions DEC-191..193. During close-out artifact authoring, a final pull surfaced that a parallel sandbox session had already pushed ses_062.json (PI-025 prior-conversations backfill, DEC-191..197) and its apply prompt; the close-out rebased a second time to SES-063 and DEC-198..200. The narrative and the renamed files (ses_063.json, CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-063.md) reflect the second rebase. The apply prompt's pre-flight re-verifies against the live API in case a third concurrent close-out arrives between now and apply time.

(2) Pre-flight reading. CLAUDE.md (root), methodology-code-change-lifecycle.md v1.0, governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md v1.0, governance-schema-specs/work_ticket.md v1.0, governance-schema-specs/reference_book.md v1.1, and crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/vocab.py read end-to-end before drafting. The other four governance schema specs (workstream, conversation, close_out_payload, deposit_event) spot-checked for cross-spec precedent context.

(3) Plan articulated and approved before drafting. Per ARCHITECTURE mode, the conversation laid out how each of the seven kickoff design questions would be handled and surfaced the calls that genuinely needed Doug's input. All seven decided autonomously per ARCHITECTURE mode's decide-and-announce posture; none individually passed the two-part stop-the-flow test (real downstream impact AND two viable options with meaningfully different outcomes), as the methodology document had already pre-decided the architectural shape and the remaining work was rendering decisions into the standard format.

(4) DEC-198 — Status-free documentary lifecycle. The methodology's v0.8 field inventory (§3.1) has no commit_status column. The reference_book spec (DEC-137 source) established that documentary-shaped lifecycles inherit base timestamps only, but reference_book still carries a three-value status field (active → archived / superseded). Commit goes further: no status field at all. The spec articulates this as a refinement of DEC-137 — documentary lifecycles MAY drop the status field entirely when no operationally meaningful states exist — and frames it as MAY-not-MUST so future documentary entities with even a two-status distinction continue to follow reference_book's precedent.

(5) DEC-199 — FK-over-references-edge deviation. The methodology §3.1 chose a foreign-key column (commit_conversation_id) for the commit→conversation relationship, and the audit queries in methodology §6.1 and §6.2 JOIN through the FK directly. This deviates from DEC-124's cross-spec precedent (references-edge over FK for parent-child governance relationships). The spec articulates the deviation explicitly with three justifications: frequency of traversal (every audit query walks commit→conversation; JOIN cost compounds linearly with commit count), cardinality (every commit has exactly one producing conversation, which FK + NOT NULL expresses cleanly), and DEC-187 reinforcement (the conversation-scoped accounting unit principle makes ownership mandatory by the model; FK enforces mandatory ownership at the schema level). The deviation is framed as the sibling of DEC-133's frequency-justified-deferral logic — the same volume-of-traversal reasoning, applied in the opposite direction.

(6) DEC-200 — Four new cross-spec precedents. The spec elevates four precedents successor governance specs may inherit: (a) status-free documentary lifecycles (per DEC-198's refinement of DEC-137); (b) natural-key lookup endpoints via GET /{plural}/by-{key}/{value} — introduced by GET /commits/by-sha/{sha} for SHA-based lookup, generalizes to any future entity with a strong natural key distinct from its V2 identifier; (c) JSON-array columns for variable-cardinality scalar lists — introduced by commit_parent_shas (0/1/2 elements), generalizes to any 0-to-N scalar list intrinsic to the entity's identity that doesn't warrant a separate normalized table; (d) conversation-scoped accounting unit principle — restating DEC-187 (settled by methodology authoring at SES-061) in spec-inheritable form. The fourth is the most consequential structurally because it generalizes the engagement-as-accounting-unit principle to any future entity type representing external artifacts (a network response, a deployed config, a database backup).

(7) Routine design choices decided per ARCHITECTURE mode and announced. Four-digit identifier width CM-NNNN (vs the three-digit governance default of CM-NNN), justified by expected cardinality (~700 historical + ongoing development volume). Client-side identifier assignment per CLAUDE.md line 62 (deviation from spec-guide §6's server-side default, consistent with every other prefixed-identifier entity in V2). SHA validation as ^[0-9a-f]{40}$ with lowercase enforced; SHA-256 migration anticipation captured as §3.8.2 open question. Repository validation: non-empty trimmed, no whitespace, no path separators, no scheme prefix; no enum constraint (new repos added as encountered). commit_committed_at preserves ISO 8601 offset (deviation from base UTC) because committer-local time is informational signal. Soft-delete-with-restore preferred over born-terminal append-only (contrast with deposit_event) because mis-ingestion correction is a real path that born-terminal would forbid. Commits panel under Governance sidebar group with Repository column and Repository filter combo (natural addition paralleling work_ticket's Kind column and close_out_payload's Status filter combo precedents); default sort commit_committed_at descending (audit-log convention paralleling deposit_event's deviation from V2's default ascending).

(8) Thirteen acceptance criteria captured in §3.7, above the spec-guide §7.1 floor of ten.

(9) Five open questions captured in §3.8 with explicit category tags: derived endpoint URL shapes [for build-planning], SHA-256 migration anticipation [for retroactive backfill or future user-interface version], commit_author_kind attribution discipline [for future user-interface version], branch-pinning queries [for retroactive backfill], sub-grouping in the Governance sidebar [for build-planning].

(10) PI-029 Prompt A authored at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-pi-029-A-commits-table-and-vocab.md. Scope: one Alembic migration (revision 0012) adding the commits table, extending refs.source_type/target_type CHECKs to admit 'commit', updating refs.relationship_kind CHECK in two steps (interim admits both 'blocks' and 'blocked_by'; data migration UPDATEs the two REF-0357/REF-0358 rows; final removes 'blocks'), extending change_log.entity_type CHECK; one vocab.py update adding 'commit' to ENTITY_TYPES, adding 'resolves' / 'addresses' / 'blocked_by' to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS, removing 'blocks', adding the three new _kinds_for_pair clauses, removing the legacy 'blocks'-emitting clauses; three migration-mechanics tests verifying table creation, blocks→blocked_by data migration, and vocab integrity. Subsequent prompts (B access layer, C REST endpoints, D tests) emerge from this prompt's review and a future PI-029 build-planning conversation.

(11) PI-028 stays Open at this conversation's close per methodology §9. The resolves_planning_items payload section the methodology specifies does not yet ship (PI-030 work), so this conversation cannot resolve its own planning item via the new mechanism. PI-033's back-fill resolves PI-028 retroactively. Honesty of "the methodology specifies a model the schema does not yet support" matters more than visual neatness of a flipped status.

(12) Close-out payload and apply prompt produced. This payload (ses_063.json) and its apply prompt (CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-063.md) cover only the three governance decisions and one is_about edge from the session to PI-028; PI-029 Prompt A is committed separately for Doug to execute via Claude Code after this close-out applies.
session.artifacts_produced: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-schema-specs/commit.md v1.0 (this conversation's primary deliverable; 427 lines, nine-subsection format per governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-pi-029-A-commits-table-and-vocab.md (Claude Code execution prompt for PI-029's structural foundation slice); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_063.json (this payload); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-063.md (apply prompt for this close-out); three decisions DEC-198, DEC-199, DEC-200; four references (three decided_in edges plus one is_about edge SES-063→PI-028). PI-028's reference_book record (the commit.md spec captured as a reference_book governance record) is NOT authored in this conversation; PI-033's back-fill handles all reference_book records for the methodology-and-schema documents authored before PI-030's close-out payload schema extension ships.
session.in_flight_at_end: PI-029 schema-migration Prompt A queued for Claude Code execution. After the SES-063 close-out applies, Doug runs the Prompt A apply locally; on success, the commits table exists in the CRMBUILDER engagement database, the refs.relationship_kind CHECK admits the three new kinds, the two existing 'blocks' rows have migrated to 'blocked_by', and vocab.py knows about 'commit' and the three new kinds. PI-029 slices B (access-layer CRUD), C (REST endpoints), D (tests) emerge from Prompt A's review and a future PI-029 build-planning conversation; this conversation does not author them. PI-030 (close-out payload extension and apply script update — adds the four new sections commits/work_tickets/resolves_planning_items/addresses_planning_items to the payload format), PI-031 (Commits panel UI), and PI-033 (historical commit and work_ticket back-fill plus retroactive resolves edges plus is_about→addresses migration plus blocks→blocked_by row migration finalization) remain Open and queued. PI-028 stays Open at this conversation's close per methodology §9 — PI-033 resolves it retroactively once the resolves_planning_items payload section ships in PI-030. Next Claude.ai conversation: depends on prioritization between (a) PI-029 build-planning (settles the URL shapes for the derived endpoints from commit.md §3.8.1 plus the access-layer cardinality rules for the new resolves/addresses edges), (b) PI-030 build-planning (settles the close-out payload schema extension and the enumerate_commits.py helper design), or (c) deferred audit-v1.2 build slices A through F that were queued by SES-058's workstream-establishing conversation. Doug's call at next conversation kickoff.

decisions.title: Commit lifecycle is documentary and status-free; refines DEC-137 — documentary lifecycles MAY drop the status field entirely when no operationally meaningful states exist
decisions.context: The Code Change Lifecycle methodology (methodology-code-change-lifecycle.md §3.1) defined the commit entity's v0.8 field inventory with no commit_status column. The PI-028 kickoff named this as Q1, asking whether the spec should confirm a documentary lifecycle posture per DEC-137 (reference_book's documentary-shape precedent) or surface an unanticipated workflow consideration. DEC-137 itself was settled at SES-050 (the reference_book schema-design conversation) and established that documentary-shaped lifecycles inherit base timestamps only — but reference_book, the precedent's original instance, still carries a three-value status field (active → archived / superseded). Commit goes further than reference_book by carrying no status field at all. The question for this conversation: does the absence of a status field warrant naming a refinement of DEC-137 as its own cross-spec precedent, or is it an unremarkable application of the existing precedent on commit's facts?
decisions.decision: Lock the commit lifecycle as status-free documentary. The commits table has no commit_status column, no transitions, no terminals. The entity either exists in the engagement database or doesn't; soft-delete-with-restore (per V2 base behavior) is the only state-change mechanism. Articulate this in commit.md §3.4 as a refinement of DEC-137: documentary-shaped lifecycles MAY drop the status field entirely when no operationally meaningful states exist. The refinement is intentionally permissive (MAY, not MUST); future documentary entities with even a two-status distinction (e.g., active/archived) continue to follow reference_book's precedent. The refinement is elevated to a cross-spec precedent (one of the four named in DEC-200) so successor governance specs can inherit the option.
decisions.rationale: Two reasons. (a) The underlying artifact has no workflow. A git commit is an immutable observed fact: there is no 'active' → 'archived' analogue (a commit doesn't become inauthentic when its message is supplanted by a later commit — the two coexist in the DAG), and there is no 'superseded' analogue (git's reset and revert operations don't supersede commits, they add new commits). The absence of a meaningful workflow on the underlying artifact is the absence of a meaningful workflow on its governance record; carrying a placeholder commit_status enum with values like active/reverted would be schema noise without semantics. (b) The refinement is worth naming explicitly because the spec-guide §3.4 names lifecycle as one of nine required subsections and the spec-guide §3.7 enumerates lifecycle-related acceptance criteria; a successor spec author reading the existing six v0.7 governance specs would see status fields everywhere (workstream, conversation, work_ticket, close_out_payload all have rich workflows; reference_book and deposit_event have status fields too despite their non-workflow shapes) and might default to authoring a status field even when none is operationally meaningful. The named refinement gives the successor author a precedent to cite when their entity also has no operational states.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option B — carry a placeholder commit_status enum with values like active/reverted/archived to maintain parity with the other six governance entities, even though none of the values are meaningful in v0.8. Rejected because placeholder columns with no semantics are schema noise; the v0.8 fields list deliberately omits commit_status, and the methodology document §3.1 anticipated the absence by listing all candidate fields explicitly. Option C — name the status-free posture as a one-off deviation in commit.md §3.4 without elevating it to a cross-spec precedent. Rejected because the methodology workstream is on a trajectory to author additional governance entity types (a future commit-attribution-kind entity, a future database-row-change entity, an api-request entity if such a workstream lands); precedents named explicitly accelerate those conversations. Option D — invert the framing and treat reference_book's three-status documentary lifecycle as the deviation rather than commit's no-status documentary lifecycle. Rejected because reference_book's three-status pattern (active → archived / superseded) is itself meaningful per its operational use (a PRD can be authoritative or archived or superseded by a newer PRD), and reference_book's precedent has already been inherited by no spec other than DEC-137's lifecycle category boundary; demoting it would require a reference_book.md spec revision without any operational benefit.
decisions.consequences: Three downstream effects. (1) The commits table has 15 columns (12 substantive + 3 governance-standard timestamps) instead of 16 (no commit_status). (2) PI-029's Alembic migration omits any commit_status CHECK constraint, simplifying the table-creation block. (3) Successor governance schema specs that find themselves authoring an entity with no operationally meaningful status field now have a named cross-spec precedent (DEC-200's first inheritable precedent, sourced from this decision) to cite. The refinement does not change reference_book's posture, does not change any v0.7 entity's posture, and does not block the future addition of a commit_status enum if real-use signal warrants — a future v0.9 migration would add the column additively without violating this decision.

decisions.title: Model commit→conversation as a foreign-key column on the commits table, not as a references-table edge; deviation from DEC-124 on frequency-justified-denormalization grounds
decisions.context: DEC-124 (settled at SES-048, the workstream schema-design conversation) established the cross-spec precedent that parent-child governance relationships use references-table edges, not foreign-key columns. The precedent has been inherited verbatim by every v0.7 governance schema spec: workstream→none, conversation→workstream via conversation_belongs_to_workstream edge, reference_book→none, work_ticket→none (inbound consumption edge from conversation, not outbound from work_ticket), close_out_payload→conversation via close_out_payload_produced_by_conversation edge, deposit_event→close_out_payload via deposit_event_applies_close_out_payload edge. The Code Change Lifecycle methodology (methodology-code-change-lifecycle.md §3.1) chose a different mechanism for the commit→conversation relationship: a foreign-key column commit_conversation_id on the commits table, with the audit queries in methodology §6.1 and §6.2 JOINing through the FK directly. This deviates from DEC-124's precedent and the methodology document does not articulate the deviation. The PI-028 kickoff named this as Q5, asking whether the spec should follow the methodology as-is with a brief noted deviation or surface the issue for explicit articulation. The conversation chose explicit articulation.
decisions.decision: Lock the FK column commit_conversation_id (TEXT, NOT NULL) on the commits table as the canonical mechanism for the commit→conversation relationship, deviating from DEC-124's references-edge precedent. No typed relationship_kind value is registered for the relationship (no commit_belongs_to_conversation kind, no commit_produced_by_conversation kind). The audit queries in methodology §6.1 and §6.2 use the FK JOIN directly. The deviation is articulated in commit.md §3.2.4 with three justifications: frequency of traversal, cardinality, DEC-187 reinforcement. The deviation is framed as the sibling of DEC-133's frequency-justified-deferral logic — the same volume-of-traversal reasoning, applied in the opposite direction. The deviation is locked for this entity type only; future high-volume governance entities (a future database_row_change entity, a future api_request entity) may apply the same frequency-justified-FK reasoning on their own facts; low-volume entities continue to default to references-edge per DEC-124.
decisions.rationale: Three reasons. (a) Frequency of traversal. Every audit query in methodology §6 walks from commit to owning conversation. The references-edge model would require one refs row per commit (refs grows linearly with commit count) and one JOIN per query (every commit-touching query becomes a two-table operation). Commits are dense: methodology §7.1 estimates several hundred historical commits per engagement plus dozens per quarter going forward, vs ~tens for work_ticket and reference_book. The FK column saves both the refs row and the JOIN at the cost of one TEXT column on the commits table. (b) Cardinality. A commit has exactly one producing conversation, always. The references-edge model is many-to-many by default; the FK column expresses one-to-many cleanly and lets the schema enforce the one-conversation constraint via NOT NULL (every commit MUST have a commit_conversation_id). Under references-edge, the constraint would require an access-layer rule (every commit MUST have an inbound edge of kind X from a conversation), which is more machinery for the same outcome. (c) DEC-187 reinforcement. The conversation-scoped accounting unit principle (DEC-187, settled by methodology authoring at SES-061) makes every commit's conversation membership mandatory by the model. FK enforces mandatory ownership at the schema level via NOT NULL; references-edge does not (a record without an edge is still a valid record absent a separate access-layer rule). The FK is the more correct semantic expression of the mandatory-ownership constraint DEC-187 establishes.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option B — model commit→conversation as a references-table edge with a new typed kind commit_produced_by_conversation (or, source-first per DEC-048, conversation_produced_commit with reversed direction). Rejected on all three grounds above: the JOIN cost compounds with commit volume; the at-most-one cardinality requires an access-layer enforcement rule; and the mandatory-ownership constraint requires the same access-layer rule. The FK with NOT NULL is structurally simpler. Option C — keep both mechanisms (FK column AND a typed references-edge), with the access layer maintaining the two in sync. Rejected as double-bookkeeping with no information gain; if both mechanisms encode the same fact, one is redundant, and the FK is the more queryable form. Option D — adopt FK but register the typed relationship_kind anyway (commit_produced_by_conversation) as documentation, with the kind never actually used in refs rows. Rejected as vocab.py noise; the value would never appear in data and would mislead future readers searching vocab for what relationships exist.
decisions.consequences: Three effects. (1) The commits table carries a NOT NULL commit_conversation_id column; the access layer validates that the named conversation exists (V2's soft-FK convention: TEXT column holding the conversation's identifier string, no SQL-level FOREIGN KEY constraint, access-layer enforcement). Mis-attribution errors surface at POST time as HTTP 422 commit_conversation_id_not_found rather than as a silently-orphaned record. (2) The cross-spec precedent landscape gains an articulated deviation pattern. Future high-volume governance entities have a named precedent (this decision) to cite when they apply the frequency-justified-FK reasoning. The DEC-124 references-edge precedent remains the default for low-volume entities. (3) The methodology document's audit queries (§6.1, §6.2) are validated by this spec — the JOIN through commit_conversation_id works exactly as the methodology describes, with no schema-level surprises waiting to surface at PI-029 implementation time.

decisions.title: Commit spec establishes four new cross-spec precedents for successor governance schema specs to inherit: status-free documentary lifecycle (refining DEC-137), natural-key lookup endpoints, JSON-array columns for variable-cardinality scalar lists, and conversation-scoped accounting unit principle as a cross-spec precedent (restating DEC-187)
decisions.context: The PI-028 kickoff named this as Q2: what (if anything) does the commit spec establish that successor governance schemas should inherit? The kickoff offered three candidate precedents and recommended elevating all three: (a) the <entity>_by-natural-key/{value} lookup endpoint pattern (introduced by GET /commits/by-sha/{sha}), (b) the JSON array column pattern for variable-cardinality scalar lists (introduced by commit_parent_shas), (c) the conversation-scoped accounting unit principle (restating DEC-187 in spec-inheritable form, since this is the first governance entity whose physical artifact lives outside the engagement database). The conversation surfaced a fourth candidate during the spec-drafting phase: the status-free documentary lifecycle refinement of DEC-137 (per DEC-198's standalone decision; the refinement is also a precedent successor specs may inherit). The question for this conversation: which (if any) of the four to elevate.
decisions.decision: Elevate all four as named cross-spec precedents in commit.md §1, with each precedent stated as MAY-not-MUST and framed for inheritance with rationale. (a) Status-free documentary lifecycle — documentary lifecycles MAY drop the status field entirely when no operationally meaningful states exist. Refines DEC-137; precedent generalizes to any future immutable-observed-fact entity. Sourced from DEC-198. (b) Natural-key lookup endpoints via GET /{plural}/by-{key}/{value} — the standard endpoint set includes identifier-based lookup at GET /{plural}/{identifier}; the natural-key variant is introduced for entities with a strong natural key distinct from the V2-assigned identifier. Ambiguous prefix returns HTTP 409 with the candidate list. Precedent generalizes to a future file_artifact entity with content-hash natural key, a future network_response entity with response-id natural key, and similar. (c) JSON-array columns for variable-cardinality scalar lists — commit_parent_shas is the first JSON array column on a governance entity table (0/1/2 elements). Precedent generalizes to any 0-to-N scalar list intrinsic to the entity's identity that is read as a unit and doesn't warrant a separate normalized table. Unbounded or queried-by-element lists still warrant separate tables (reference_book → reference_book_versions pattern remains the precedent for those). (d) Conversation-scoped accounting unit principle — restates DEC-187 (settled by methodology authoring at SES-061) in spec-inheritable form. The physical home of an artifact and the governance home of its record are independent; the governance home is always the producing conversation's engagement database; the physical home is captured by an attribute (here, commit_repository). Precedent generalizes to any future governance entity representing an external artifact (a deployed config, a database backup, a third-party service response).
decisions.rationale: Two reasons. (a) Successor governance specs benefit from named precedents to inherit. The six v0.7 governance schema specs in PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-schema-specs/ each open with an inheritance block that lists prior precedents; named precedents accelerate successor conversations by giving them well-articulated patterns to apply on their own facts (rather than re-deriving the pattern from scratch). (b) Each of the four named precedents is likely to recur. The by-sha lookup pattern especially: future entities with strong natural keys (content hashes, response IDs, API tokens) will reach for the same endpoint shape. The JSON-array-column pattern is a small enough idea that any future entity needing a bounded scalar list (a list of email addresses, a list of categorical tags) may apply it. The conversation-scoped accounting unit principle is the most consequential structurally — it generalizes the engagement-as-accounting-unit principle to any future entity type representing artifacts that physically live outside the engagement database, of which commit is the first instance.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option B — elevate only the three precedents the kickoff named, on the grounds that the fourth (status-free documentary) is already captured by DEC-198 and a separate cross-spec listing would be duplicative. Rejected because cross-spec precedents are inheritance signals to successor specs, and a successor spec author reading commit.md §1 should see the full inheritable set in one place. DEC-198 records the architectural decision; DEC-200's (a) line records the inheritability. Option C — elevate only the third (conversation-scoped accounting unit principle), on the grounds that it is the most consequential and the other three are minor patterns that don't need precedent-level naming. Rejected because the other three are not minor in the aggregate — the by-sha lookup is a non-trivial URL-design choice with ambiguous-prefix semantics; the JSON-array-column choice has implications for query patterns (JSON_EACH() vs separate-table JOIN); the status-free refinement is a real choice future specs face. All three are worth naming. Option D — elevate zero new precedents, on the grounds that commit is sufficiently different from the v0.7 governance entities that inheritance is unlikely. Rejected because the Code Change Lifecycle methodology is the start of a longer arc (PI-029 through PI-033 in scope; future commit-attribution-kind, future audit-event-record, future workspace-state-snapshot entities all plausible), and inheritance becomes more useful as the family grows.
decisions.consequences: Three effects. (1) The cross-spec precedent landscape grows by four entries. Successor governance schema specs that find themselves authoring an entity with one of the four pattern shapes have a named precedent to cite. The cumulative precedent set after this spec is: DEC-124 (references-edge over FK; deviation in DEC-199), DEC-125 (terminal-states-are-terminal), DEC-126 (per-status timestamps for workflow lifecycles), DEC-133 (typed sequencing introduced when frequency justifies), DEC-137 (documentary lifecycle category; refined by DEC-198), DEC-143 (terminal-state consumption requires inbound edge), DEC-156 (born-terminal append-only), DEC-158 (multi-event-per-target-record), and now DEC-200's four. (2) PI-029's structural foundation slice (Prompt A) implements the schema-level realization of three of the four precedents: status-free table shape (no commit_status column); the JSON column type for commit_parent_shas; the by-sha endpoint stub in vocab.py is not directly relevant to Prompt A but the endpoint pattern is documented in commit.md §3.5 for subsequent slices. The conversation-scoped accounting unit principle does not need a schema-level implementation — it is satisfied by the access layer routing every record to the producing conversation's engagement database, which already happens per the v0.7 multi-tenancy fix (SES-045, DEC-167 et seq.). (3) Future build-planning conversations have a clearer inheritance map. The PI-029 build-planning conversation (not yet held; will follow Prompt A's execution and review) opens against this spec's §3.8 open questions plus DEC-200's four precedents as starting context.
